events.text: Karen Jones, 46-year-old Asian female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the Base of tongue. Clinical staging: T2N1M0, AJCC Stage III. ICD-10: C10.9. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx.
CT neck with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 3 dysphagia. Speech pathology evaluation. Modified diet consistency. PEG tube nutrition supplementation.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
CT neck with contrast performed for restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Clinical visit for results review after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Completed definitive radiation: IMRT 70 Gy in 35 fractions to primary site and involved nodes, 56 Gy to elective nodal volumes. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT NECK WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Date of Study: 2023-04-16

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Specimen ID: S2023-16977
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-18
Date of Report: 2023-04-21

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Karen Jones - Base of tongue" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.2 x 2.2 x 1.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Radiation Oncology

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Date: 2023-04-27
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Jones is a 46-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue, III. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated). Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hypothyroidism
- atrial fibrillation

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Nurse (retired)
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg daily
- Apixaban 5 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild cough, mild dyspnea on exertion, dizziness, constipation. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/84, HR 92, RR 16, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 78.8 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.0, Hgb 12.4, Plt 235, Cr 0.93

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
46-year-old female with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. initial oncology consultation.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue, Stage III
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend definitive concurrent chemoradiation
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Radiation Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Date of Report: 2023-05-01
Specimen: Base of tongue (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated)
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.3 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  HPV HPV-16 positive (p16 IHC positive) - Viral [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PIK3CA p.E545K - Missense (Exon 9)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 613x coverage.
Tumor content: 68%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Radiation Oncology

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Date: 2023-06-05
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: cycle 2 assessment

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Jones is a 46-year-old female with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Appetite is decreased but maintaining weight.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: decreased appetite, dry mouth, mild cough, night sweats. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 112/87, HR 69, RR 14, Temp 97.6F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 92.1 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.6, Hgb 9.1, Plt 119, Cr 0.72

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
46-year-old female with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) - cycle 2 assessment
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Continue antiemetic regimen
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Radiation Oncology

MRI NECK WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Karen Jones
MRN: patient_3bea6149b0c4
Date of Study: 2024-01-15

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
OROPHARYNX: Enhancing mass centered in the base of tongue measuring 2.6 x 1.8 cm with extension to the vallecula.
NECK: Level IIA lymphadenopathy on the left, largest node 2.5 cm with heterogeneous enhancement.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology
